# People

Topics in the **People** category of the *the country japan* knowledge base.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [Hirohito](hirohito.md) | The 124th Emperor of Japan (r. 1926–1989), whose Showa era spanned imperial expansion, defeat in World War II, and the postwar rise to the world's second-largest economy. |
| [Murasaki Shikibu](murasaki-shikibu.md) | The 11th-century Heian court lady and author of *The Tale of Genji*, widely regarded as the world's first novel and the supreme masterpiece of classical Japanese literature. |
| [Katsushika Hokusai](katsushika-hokusai.md) | The *ukiyo-e* master (1760–1849) whose *Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji* and "The Great Wave off Kanagawa" defined Japanese visual art worldwide and helped ignite European *Japonisme*. |
| [Tokugawa Ieyasu](tokugawa-ieyasu.md) | The third great unifier (1543–1616) who won at Sekigahara, founded the Tokugawa shogunate, and built the *bakuhan* institutions that gave Japan 265 years of Edo-period peace. |
| [Empress Meiji](empress-meiji.md) | Empress consort Shōken (1849–1914), wife of Emperor Meiji—a symbol of Japan's Westernizing modernization, patron of women's education and the Red Cross, and a prolific *waka* poet enshrined at Meiji Shrine. |
| [Abe Shinzo](abe-shinzo.md) | Japan's longest-serving prime minister (in office 2006–2007 and 2012–2020)—architect of "Abenomics," the reinterpretation of Article 9, and the Free and Open Indo-Pacific, assassinated in 2022. |
| [Yukio Mishima](yukio-mishima.md) | Japan's most celebrated and controversial postwar author (1925–1970)—the novelist of *Confessions of a Mask*, *The Temple of the Golden Pavilion*, and the *Sea of Fertility* tetralogy, whose ultranationalist Shield Society and 1970 *seppuku* at Ichigaya shocked the nation. |
| [Minamoto no Yoritomo](minamoto-no-yoritomo.md) | The founder of the Kamakura shogunate (1147–1199)—whose victory over the Taira in the Genpei War and 1192 appointment as *sei-i taishōgun* created Japan's first warrior government and inaugurated nearly seven centuries of samurai rule. |
| [Ashikaga Takauji](ashikaga-takauji.md) | The samurai general (1305–1358) who overthrew Emperor Go-Daigo's Kenmu Restoration to found the Ashikaga (Muromachi) shogunate—perpetuating warrior rule and fostering the Zen-infused arts of the Muromachi age. |
| [Emperor Meiji](emperor-meiji.md) | The 122nd Emperor (1852–1912), whose 45-year reign presided over Japan's transformation from a feudal domain into a modern constitutional and imperial power—the abolition of the samurai, the Iwakura Mission, the 1889 Constitution, industrialization, and victories over China and Russia. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
